metadata.greeting: This weekend in Hackney looks amazing! I've found lots of free and interactive activities perfect for a 3-year-old, from exploring animals at city farms to getting creative with crafts at local museums. It's going to be a fun-filled couple of days!

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: Great for quiet time and introducing new books to a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes from Hackney Central.
transport.tip: Check term times before you go.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Interactive museum designed for children with hands-on activities and exhibitions focused on design, imagination and play
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed with children in mind, offering plenty to keep them entertained. Offers activities and play spaces perfect for toddlers

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green, then a 2-minute walk
transport.tip: Avoid peak hours as it can get crowded

activities.title: Mudlarks Children's Gallery
activities.description: Interactive gallery for children to learn about the history of London's Docklands through play.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed with children in mind, lots of hands-on activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shadwell, then DLR to West India Quay.
transport.tip: Check for special events or closures before visiting.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A wooden playground with a pond theme, perfect for toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for toddlers with a safe and engaging play environment.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford.
transport.tip: Combine with a walk around the Olympic Park.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Immersive play space with story worlds and a story garden playground.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers immersive play spaces and storytelling sessions, perfect for engaging a 3-year-old's imagination

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford.
transport.tip: Check for baby storytelling sessions.

activities.title: Library Club
activities.description: A weekly club with reading corner and diverse book pairing services for kids.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a calm space for reading and discovering new stories.

transport.fromHackney: Local buses.
transport.tip: Check for specific events each week.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: Meet farmyard animals in the heart of Hackney.
activities.whyItWorks: A chance to see animals up close without a long journey.

transport.fromHackney: Walk or cycle.
transport.tip: Check for feeding times.

activities.title: Free Activities at the V&A
activities.description: The V&A offers free activities for families every day, including drop-in design and play sessions.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers creative and engaging activities for all ages, sparking imagination and learning through play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Notting Hill Gate, then the Circle Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check the V&A website for the daily schedule.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Search for giant Easter eggs hidden around the zoo, each revealing a secret letter to uncover a hidden code.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines the excitement of an Easter egg hunt with the fun of seeing animals at the zoo.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Euston, then a short bus ride to the Zoo
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance to avoid queues.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, fascinating specimens and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: The dinosaurs and interactive exhibits are always a hit with this age group. Free entry makes it accessible.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Green Park, then the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Interactive exhibits that let kids discover how the world works.
activities.whyItWorks: Wonderlab offers hands-on experiences with experiment demonstrations, talks, activities, and educational play zones. The Garden is an interactive space for younger children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Green Park, then the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book Wonderlab tickets in advance.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Small zoo with rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Completely free to enter and home to wallabies, lemurs and Scottish wildcat.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Gospel Oak, then the Overground to Golders Green.
transport.tip: Combine with a picnic in the park.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.price: Included with entry to the ship
activities.description: Explore the historic ship and find hidden eggs with letters to unscramble a nautical code, and claim a chocolatey treat
activities.whyItWorks: Exciting adventure to discover fascinating facts about Cutty Sark's history and epic voyages. Enjoyable for the whole family

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shadwell, then the DLR to Cutty Sark.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance to avoid queues.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace Easter Events
activities.description: Easter-themed egg hunts and seasonal workshops around the historic palace and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines royal history, spring flowers, and fun family-friendly activities

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Clapham Junction, then a train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Explore natural history, musical instruments and anthropology exhibits. Also has gardens and aquarium.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of indoor and outdoor activities, including a Natural History Gallery and beautiful gardens to explore.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Check out the aquarium and animal farm for an additional fee.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers
activities.description: Giant Easter egg trail, meet the Easter Bunny, indoor playbarn, outdoor adventure play villages, zoo and farm animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of activities to keep a 3-year-old entertained, including animals and play areas.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and train to Hounslow, then bus
transport.tip: Book online for cheaper tickets.

activities.title: Family Fun Day: Build Your Own Cocoon
activities.description: Make your own foldable space of safety using fabric and natural dyes.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative, hands-on activity that is suitable for all ages.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Croydon.
transport.tip: Check for specific times for the event.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Follow the trail and match the names of influential characters to the Lindt gold bunny statues.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun and interactive way to explore the palace grounds with a chocolate prize at the end.

transport.fromHackney: Train from Liverpool Street to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book in advance online

activities.title: Royal Mews Easter Egg Expedition
activities.description: Follow an Easter trail around the working stables at Buckingham Palace.
activities.whyItWorks: Explore historic carriages and the courtyard while looking for clues.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Victoria Station.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: A performance of Alice in Wonderland.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun and imaginative show that will capture the imagination of children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Hammersmith.
transport.tip: Check the age suitability before booking.

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail
activities.description: Discover a variety of illustrated animals nestled amongst the blooming habitats of the reserve, identify the letter on each one and complete the secret word in the activity booklet.
activities.whyItWorks: Combine a walk in nature with a fun activity that links to a familiar story.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Walthamstow.
transport.tip: Suitable for younger children.

activities.title: Spring Family Fun
activities.price: Some paid activities and some free
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity for families at the Southbank Centre includes super shows, musical adventures, amazing puppetry and lots of free fun in the Easter break.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a wide range of activities to suit different interests and budgets.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for the full programme.

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: A play space made out of repurposed materials.
activities.whyItWorks: A unique and sustainable play space that encourages creativity and imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for the full programme.
